sections.heading: Deep Dive: Phone Calls & Email Etiquette
sections.body: Let's go deeper into Phone Calls & Email Etiquette. This section covers the internals and advanced concepts that separate beginners from proficient developers.

sections.heading: Common Patterns & Best Practices
sections.body:
Here are the best practices for Phone Calls & Email Etiquette:

1. Start Simple: Begin with the simplest implementation that works, then add complexity as needed.

2. Follow Conventions: Stick to established patterns and conventions in your codebase. Consistency makes code maintainable.

3. Test Your Work: Always test your implementation. Write tests that cover both happy paths and edge cases.

4. Document Decisions: Add comments that explain WHY, not WHAT. Future you (and teammates) will thank you.

5. Review and Refactor: Code reviews and regular refactoring keep code quality high.

sections.heading: Practice Task
sections.body: Apply what you've learned about Phone Calls & Email Etiquette. Try building a small project that uses these concepts. Start with the basics and gradually add features.

summary: Phone Calls & Email Etiquette is an essential concept that you'll use throughout your development journey. Professional phone manner and email writing basics. Practice the examples in this lesson and apply them to your own projects.
